title: The History of Aviation
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The dream of human flight (0) goes back thousands of years, but it was not until the early 20th century that it became a reality. The Wright brothers are (1) credited with making the first powered flight in 1903. Their achievement (2) paved the way for rapid developments in aviation technology. During the First World War, aircraft were (3) put to use for military purposes, which accelerated their development. Commercial aviation (4) took off in the 1920s, making travel across continents possible for the first time. Pilots became celebrities, and long-distance flights (5) captured the public's imagination. Jet engines, introduced after the Second World War, (6) brought about a revolution in air travel. Today, millions of passengers (7) rely on aircraft to travel for business and leisure. Despite concerns about environmental impact, the aviation industry continues to grow as engineers (8) work on developing more sustainable technologies for the future.

example.explanation: 'Goes back' es la expresión correcta para indicar que algo se remonta en el tiempo.

questions.explanation: 'Credited with' significa reconocer a alguien por un logro.
'Paved the way for' es la expresión idiomática para facilitar desarrollos futuros.
'Put to use' significa utilizar algo para un propósito específico.
'Took off' significa despegar o empezar a tener éxito.
'Captured the imagination' es la colocación correcta para fascinar al público.
'Brought about' significa causar o provocar un cambio importante.
'Rely on' es la expresión más común para depender de algo.
'Work on' significa trabajar en el desarrollo de algo.
